inbound_signal: DKR-0 review rejected three rows and two wall sources; DKR-2 review could not execute either replay and found signal-error ambiguity; DKR-3 v2 now repairs its stale evidence; DKR-1 v3 adds multiple-error aggregation.
decision_delta: Dispatched only evidence repairs for DKR-0, DKR-1, DKR-2, and DKR-3; kept the breaking flag open and all product changes paused.
expected_effect: Reach independently replayable architecture decisions before opening the narrow cleanup resolution and any progression lane.
evidence_delta: DKR-0 formal coverage passes but three rows and two wall sources fail; DKR-1 v3 reads 11 of 11 cleanup cases; DKR-2 independent behavior reads no result because replay failed; DKR-3 v2 resolves 11 of 11 hashes and preserves all prior behavior.
problem_restatement: Make every architectural choice replayable from the repository root, preserve original errors under cancellation, and distinguish declared graph activation from hidden dynamic or physical effect edges.
alternatives_considered: Accept conclusions whose validator harness failed
Fix product code while correcting the evidence later
Repair each proof at discovery level and revalidate before progression
assumptions_at_risk: Signal-only cancellation remains sufficient after original-error preservation
Dynamic WorkerRegistry dispatch and direct child_process can be isolated behind explicit graph edges
All corrected DKR checkpoints can hold their active walls from direct sources
dkr_decision: Continue all four bounded proof repairs; no CKR or PKR promotion and no committing move until independent validators accept the corrected checkpoints and the breaking resolution is recorded.

hallucination_sweep.claim: A validator record is independent evidence even when its replay never ran.

missing_context_sweep.verdict: rejected; unrelated original errors require an explicit preservation case
